pages.text:
swelled out. French bread. Bugs, Surfs, sunset, sunshine. village, Barn, marina. Wonderful campsite for children, - triple slide into pool, games, climbing frame. Log cabins. Tent cabins.

__Wednesday__
We set off expecting to cycle on a Voit Verte near [?Taijf] Sault - Brenoy, but when we stopped to Viarthona [?Viathona] buy bread at Sault-Breny while I was photographing the bridge, I saw cyclists. So we went on a lovely, gentle bike ride up + down + along the island. On the island there was a specially laid out canoeing course, 2 of them, with rocks + rapids.
On to have lunch near alot of lakes at Dombes Abbey. The monks were invited in 1860, to drain the lakes because the average age was only 20-30. Cistercians. After lunch we went in - they had a prayer room of icons, remembering the 6 monks killed in N Africa. A very plain church. Espaliered apple trees. One monk

captions: Cistercian Abbey Notre Dame des Dombes 01330 le Plantay
pages.text:
was taken off to Belsen for working with the Resistance + got the Croix de Guerre. 2 other monks were killed.

We shopped - for England, pisacio, pate + garlic, + reached St. Jean de Vaux.

We have waited 25 years to have supper in the St. Jean de Vaux restaurant, but it has always been closed in August. Now we are here in May, but the restaurant has now shut down! So we had moules & frites at the campsite.
"Thursday May 11"
A bit of rain in the night. Increasing rain in the morning. Isaiah keeps not [?being]. We walked round the village & up past the water tanks. The path has eroded badly. Back to the van for lunch.
It rained & rained & rained all afternoon. Stilla [?failure] to contact Isaiah. Finally his wife rings, & the campsite owner gets hold of her & she says Isaiah has semi-retired. Their son works on cars & the vineyard is closing. He sends us to Monneret (La Fosse 06 72 92 98 70/03 85 45 1756 in St. Marc de Vaux, just beyond the St. Jean de Vaux graveyard). Simon made an appt. at 5pm. So we drove there & bought 60 bottles of Cremant de Bourgogne & some red too. Very nice people. In the evening it stopped raining.

[...in] Friday We left promptly to go north to near Arras. When we reached the village which should have had a campsite we couldn't find the site. So we spent the night on the 3 hopeurs / hypers on the Grand Place in Arras. We had to move the van into the middle of the square because of the market the next day.
We had supper here. →
[glued-in business card: Le Petit Rat Porteur / Restaurant, Brasserie, Cuisine de tradition / Salle en cave : possibilité réunion de famille, repas d'affaires... / Christelle et Michel CAUCHY / 11, rue de la Taillerie - 62000 ARRAS / Tél. 03 21 51 29 70 / Email : lepetitratporteur@orange.fr]
They would do a family reunion meal (38 seats in basement) if we had a reunion at Alexander Claud Kirby's grave next August. (only last week in August, because closed earlier in month.)
Saturday We drove to Vimy to buy bread. So many towns & villages have lost their bakeries. Vimy ridge steep. Lots of Canadian [?&] memorials. On to the Tunnel.

We had an uneventful tunnel crossing, thank goodness, & reached Charles at 3 as predicted. But unfortunately as Simon reversed into the space outside their house in Frenchay Rd. there was a crunch. The woman who drove up behind didn't realise we were reversing.

     Charles & Jenny were well & so was Tinder. We took him on a walk to photograph the scratch on the woman's car (it wasn't there so Charles photographed it later). & then reached Westfield Rd at 6.30. A super, long holiday.

__June 2nd Friday__
8.45. Conrad, Eleni, Tinder, Simon & I set off for Telford where Simon met with Chris Sparks to discuss conveyor belts with [?Tamer] for the hop barn for 1½ hours. On to Berrington Hall NT while we napped. There

Tinder went wild straining at the sheep until we got into the grounds. The children played in the playground & we had cake in the courtyard & then into the shop/cafe when it rained. Tinder sat under the table umbrella.

     On to the house Peter has rented for Hay festival w/end. It sleeps 5. Charles & Jenny, John & Kay came, but John & Kay 6ft straight away because Kay had a stiff shoulder. land lady nice but fussy. Sends the van & S, C, E & E & T away to sleep in a farmer's field. Peter had supper with us & then the children wanted to sleep at Peter's house, so at 8.45. he took them there, then brought them back at 9.45. because the land lady has a mezzanine floor & worries about children. (When we cdn't find her via postcode, Simon had said "all we saw was dilapidated farm buildings"). Then we all went to sleep, phew! & the chil. slept till 7.10.

__Sat__ Peter & Charles arrived for an outdoor leisurely breakfast. Then Peter & Simon & Eve set off for the Hay Book festival (30 years) & parked in a field (£3). We wandered in the

festival. Peter read to the children in the Maker Take tent. Charles + Jenny joined us. Simon + Peter went to a talk "War" by AC Grayling. Eve bought books + a puzzle for Conrad. Jenny + Charles, Conrad, Eleni + Eve just got back to the van before rain fell. We did the dragon puzzle + read. S + P returned. Some of us returned to the campsite. John + Jenny went to talks.
[illegible] In the evening Simon put Co K. to bed. Charles + Jenny spent the night in the van with them (pillows not comfy.) Peter took Eve back to Hay. He + John went to one lecture, while Eve went to "Vikings + Sagas" - brilliant - by Eleanor Barradough. Peter John + Eve had supper (veg terrine + duck) + Simon arrived. We walked by the river + at 11 pm went to St. Mary's church for a candlelit concert. We had to stand outside rather too long but were rewarded by impressive and sensitive cello playing interspersed with poetry. Jeanette Winterton was sitting in front of us.

__Sunday June 4th__
   We had spent the night in the barn which Peter had rented. Then we had a very crowded breakfast in the van where I burnt half the toast.
   Everyone else left + we cleared up + visited Abbey Dore (founded 1147) + had coffee, tea. We have forgotten the veg. knife, coffee pot, shaving brush, butter.
   Then home.

__Friday July 28th__        __Bromyard__
Eve drove red jazz to farm. Simon drove camping van + parked in farm yard. Saturday Hooray! Charles' + Jenny's wedding at Belmont Abbey. Reception at the top of the hill at the farm. Charles' van got a puncture at the Abbey. The trailer (for taking people up the hill) axel broke. A van got a flat tire. Cars skidded, collided with trees. The rain rained + rained. Mud mud mud. Absolutely glorious party.

Sunday Bryony gave us lunch & we drove home to take the Mitchcocks to St. George's.

__Friday August 4th__
8 am we set off to the factory. Picked up lights. Dropped off dog. On to NE of Norwich to deliver lights for a depot for Lanzarote time share. Reached Seamark after 5 pm. Swam Supper at Golf Club with Charles & Jenny, John & Kaz, Peter, Nana, Conrad & Eleni. Peter treated us. Slept at Seamark. Got cystitis after long journey.

Saturday [?Hodes] Moved to sleep outside Annie's. Mitsuba's & Taiki's wedding reception. Simon went on [?Meave] with Jenny & Peter & Conrad & Eleni. Eve helped with hall decorations. Lunch at Dolphin. 4 pm party. Coffee, meal, dancing dancing. Didn't feel well. Fortunately John did[d]

got a prescription off David.

Sunday milled around, chatted. Lunch in Dolphin garden. Lovely watching children. [?Ptr] Thomas has a large tent. Took Conta & Wanda to station. Simon suddenly suggested visiting Pernille & Kit.

Hamburgers on the way home. Junct 6 blocked so got off onto M42 & past airport & Chad. Home at 10.

__Wed 16th Aug__
Simon, Eve, Tinder left 86 at 10. Off to farm & then camping with John & Kay to celebrate John's 30th birthday on Saturday. It's Simon's second to last visit to look at the new hop barn before the hop harvest begins on Aug 31st. He & William worked on the zig-zagging conveyor belts with Chris & 2 other Polish workmen. Bryony was out singing - a choir for tutoring choral conductors from all over the world. Eve

captions: Glass doors Axbridge Church designed & engraved by Tracey Sheppard F G E
pages.text:
Glass doors Axbridge Church designed & engraved by Tracey Sheppard F G E

Played with Joseph, Seb & Nina & new nanny, house. Johnnie was there but left after lunch. I also unloaded & my tea lady stuff into shed & cleaner big black tubs from Charles' wedding.

St. John the Baptist Axbridge

Thursday. Joseph & Seb came into the van for breakfast. Seb switched on water. Joseph put bread to toast on grill tray. Nina finally joined us when [?going/doing]

worked out that she would only come if there was toast. Louisa took children off to a nature park. Simon & Eve looked at Humbolt, No 2 cabin, where we will stay for a fortnight during hop harvest?
	At 11 we left, [?stopped at Ledges] & went past Chepstow Racetad. We spent the night in __Almondsbury__ - a campsite within sound of M4. [?Elevenses] at Tintern Abbey.
	[tea?]
__Friday__ On to Axbridge. Elevenses just north of Clevedon. We saw Clevedon Pier. Then we parked out of town & found a deserted path into Ladye Bay (treacherous tides) & walked along the coastal path a bit. [?Lant] Lunch in the lamb? at Axbridge. We looked at the lovely church & got back to the van just before another terrific rain shower.
	On to __Greenacres__ campsite near Compton Martin. Charles & Jenny arrived at tea time, from Germany at the end of their honey moon. John & Kaz arrived in time for supper.

We had supper in the van, catermittent showers.
__Saturday__ John's 30th birthday
We went on a 4 mile walk. The roads near the campsite are tricky & dangerous, but we managed to reach a Roman Road to go up thru the woods, up the hill. Lots of roots & turns. Lunch at the pub near the campsite. After lunch we napped then John beat Eve by nearly 100. [?Kaylos] given John a gorgeous bomber jacket for his birthday.
	At 7 we set out on the 6 mile walk to the Pony & Trap, table booked for 9. John & Kay just made it. The rest of us were a few minutes late. Delicious meal. Lamb & choc pud. We got a taxi home.

__Sunday__
We walked next to one of the 2 lakes. John & Kaz left. Charles visited a Malvern [?friend]. Simon & Eve walked to St. Andrews Blagdon - lovely stained glass. Then we met up & Jenny bought us lunch at the Chew Magna pub. Tinker has behaved

perfectly. We are stopping off with papers for the Chad accountant at Alcester on the way home.
__Monday October 16th__
Eve caught an Uber to Chad, cleared the loo & we set off for supper with Charles & Jenny. Stopped off at Aldi for dog's food.

Sophie Allport

Caerleon "Fort of the Legion"

April 13th 2018
 Simon, Eve & Tinder left for Wales to visit John & Kagin their new house (11am). We stopped in Bourneville to buy buttons.
      We parked by the river park, flat land, below Ross on Wye & walked up the hill for a good lunch at Man of Ross. We bought crafts & books.
      On to Caerleon ("fort of the legion"). The amphitheatre (could seat 6,000) was excavated 1927, but the rest – granary, barracks, has only been excavated/being excavated since the '60. An interesting museum – a mock barrack room for 8 men with bunks – nice curators.
      We spent the night at a campsite – "White House Farm"* a miles' walk away. Heater in modern shower block. V. Clean.
      We have forgotten marmalade & tea towels.

__Saturday April 14th__

Off to the Kennels (Kaj's cates, Denzel, has only just started going outside.) then arrived at Penn Onn at 10.15... John & Kay will arrive from Bath soon. Charles is flying back from showing his robot for a factory to help him in Bologna & picking up Jenny at Reading to get here oby 2pm. Peter will arrive at 4 (Nurse & child in India) Koroku coming too.

After Peter arrived in a Taxi from the station (£16) we went for a walk in a big rectangle along the roads, over 4 miles.

Tinder is in Kennels. Denzel brings in mice, or rather just the intestines of mice, having eaten the rest of them. 12 mice in 6 days.

John cooked delicious beef Wellington for supper & Kay cooked Charles a delicious walnut

[margin, rotated: St. Teilo's Church, (3) National History Museum St Fagans.]

Birthday cake. We had smoked salmon on Rogentis / Irish soda bread.

The house warmed up once John & Simon had got the new oil into the boiler.

[margin, rotated: St. Teilo's Church, (3) National History Museum St Fagans. When the near-derelict church was moved from near Swansea, bits & pieces of flooring were discovered. It had been c.1520]

[photo: interior of St. Teilo's Church at the National History Museum, St Fagans — painted wooden ceiling beams and decorative tiled floor panels visible]

[photo caption, printed on right edge: Amgueddfa Werin Cymru Sain Ffagan]

Sunday Simon + Eve picked up Tinder at 9.30 so only £15. Then we went to St. Fagan's museum. The main building opens later in the year. So we looked at out door buildings that have been moved there.
    A gorse mill - Small. 2 men + 1 boy wd. take 20 min. to process a bunch of gorse - This was fed to livestock. Cd. be fed all year + grew on worst soil, so better soil cd be used for other crops.
    We went to a pub for lunch + raced home (lots of traffic) + got there with 10 minutes to spare to get to church.
    __Thursday May 3rd__
    We set off at 9, voted (Newman House) for city councillors + dropped off Tinder at Kenads. Lunch in the van at Cobham services + on to Ightham Mote. Lovely [C4] house. Beautiful linen fold panelling, paint colour in rooms good, welcoming. Well worth a visit. V.beautiful.
    On to the Plough Inn Stalisfield, where Simon has already booked supper. They have put us in the fireplace again. They are building

captions: Ightham Mote — Kent 2018 — House, garden and estate (National Trust leaflet cover, glued in)
pages.text: holiday lets in the next field. It has been cold for days but now there is nice warm evening sunshine.

We got to the Tunnel an hour early & they put us onto the next train - ahead of the Bank Holiday weekend.
We drove past Etaples + Rouen + are camping beyond Dreux at a Municipal Park at St. Remysur l'Avre. The river splits in 2 in the town.
A lovely, au warm evening walk in the Sunshine. There's an Irishman in the camp who sits in his car all day.
Delicious
    eclairs
1/3 fields oil seed rape
[illegible] [illegible]

captions: Restaurant L'Atelier de Jeanne, 3 impasse du Chateau - Meung sur Loire
pages.text:
[?Bble] Saturday.
      We shopped in Dreux + lunched overlooking R. le [?lour] a few kilometres north of Chateaudun. We've camped at le [?Floe] at Beaugency. lots of watermills, broad river. Old bridges. Church has a capitol of David & Goliath & font from 1593. A good meal (-except pudding). A nice town to wander in.

      Lovely views from the van across the river.

__Sunday__ a near-perfect day so far.
Warm sunshine in the van first thing. An 11 mile bike ride to Meung. lovely bike track. Fields, villages.
Delicious lasagna, rose + lashings of chocolate on the profiteroles at this restaurant. Frogs making a tremendous racket.
A market in Meung + beatiful Romanesque door outside church + nice Stained glass window.
Back to Beaugency. The bridge is beautifal.
Both campsites have been only £11 per night.

__Monday__ We're off to see David. Helen + he have flown there. John + Kay should be there too in the Caterham.
Lots of mills at Beaugency – "In the Town? They 've routed the water, dividing the stream in 2 before it got to the [illegible] river the water was reused.

[photo caption: Le Rû]
[French text on photo: Au fil du Rû, long de 2700 mètres, onze moulins sont installés au Moyen Age, dans les quartiers du Prateau et des Marais. La rivière, de faible débit est aménagée afin d'assurer une énergie hydraulique suffisante. En complément, dans sa partie supérieure des moulins à vent sont construits au XVIII° siècle pour pallier le manque de rendement. Ils disparaissent au début du XX° siècle. Les moulins à eau sont devenus des résidences particulières et ne sont pas accessibles au public.]

We reached David a bit later than planned. It was lovely to have a bit of the house & garden.

__Tuesday__ We managed a 14½ mile walk to Treignac lake – gorgeous old roads + scenery. Yoga helps stop the stiffness. Nice to spend time with everyone. I had a ride in the Caterha[m]
__Wednesday__ John + Kay left after Simon had driven the Caterham. They will cross at St Malo tomorrow. David, Helen, Simon + [I] went to Treignac for the bank, shoes for Helen + seeds. The others went for a shorter walk after lunch. We all did some gardening. Nasturtiums. David has cooked delicious [illegible] stew. There are 2 beautiful matched white horses from the Camargue in David's field, for a couple of weeks, mowing the grass for him. Mr. Levine is anxious – the mare has a bad hoof. If he has to put her down (21 years old) the other will have to be put down too. They are inseperable.

Thursday May 10th.
We left at 9.30 & came south, as directed by David, through Meymac, Bort, Riom, Florenc. We are camping on R. Hepault (wine growing) near caves of the Madelons. We have passed over causse, very dry limestone, gorsey not farmland. Down here it's warmer & grapes are harvested.

Friday
[?Deny] RH died on Wed. Olivia Tufnoll died [strikethrough: Thu] Friday
We are in camping d'Anglas near Brossac.
A beautiful meadow with the sound of a river.
We saw those amazing caves - Grotte dos-demoisell[?es]
Millions of years old. Just after lunch at Ganges David Fellowes rang to say Ols has died (She had died just before noon.)
We returned to the camp via a supermarket.
Simon swam. We ride bikes to the shower house.

[?Thursday] May 12th.
We walked along paths to Brossac. The castle looks beautifully built. We walked around it & looked at the old town then went down to the new town. The river [strikethrough: is] bank is full of bamboo. There's an old house & paper factory guarded by an alsation. We had lunch at L'Arboussede. A local [strikethrough: the] petanque club was also having a lunch there. Good trout.

Back at the van we just got the floor swept before rain poured down.

Why are so many of the vines left unwedded?

__Sunday__
A difficult start to the day. My loo was overflowing, so I had to bail it out. We stopped to look at an old bridge ([?2]) & church - St. [?Catherine] Iss.... & Simon lost the lens in his glasses, & it poured & poured rain & we were cold & couldn't find it. He's now driving in dark glasses. Things got better when we

silk worms
eating
mulberry
leaves

got to the Silk Museum at St. Hyppolyte du Fauve. It was fascinating & very interesting. Dreadful conditions for women. At its height in 1860. Pasteur found a cure for the silk worm [strikethrough: disease] [?disease] (p? nosema). a [?feneges].

We had the heater on during lunch (7°) & are now going to a hotel for the night. We have now gone into a huge jam, 1½ hrs had supper & are now in slow-moving traffic to our hotel at Tournon. I ended up having to drive in the dark & rain because of Simon's glasses, which was pretty fraught. The hotel room was nice. It looked onto the Rhône. [strikethrough: The] The [strikethrough: two] town seems to be in a valley surrounded by vines...

captions: The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.
pages.text:
[postcard of Tournon-sur-Rhône glued in]

The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.

Monday

Having left the hotel, we motor-wayed to Lyons & spent an hour trying to find a parking place without Success. There's a rail strike & they've blocked the main square from parking. It would have been nice to have a meal.

We have come to a place the Same distance north of Lyons (as Tournon) we're now at a rainy

__Tournous__, an important Mediaeval Monastery town.

We had a delicious supper after looking around the abbey. Simon had cheese & salad & I had murdered eggs (vinegar.) The plates were really hot. Then Simon had steak & I had chicken. Then Simon had crème brûlée & I had chocolate mousse. A really good meal in our hotel de la Paix.
__Tuesday__ Overcast & damp, but cleared up by lunch time & warmer now at tea time. We saw the abbey again [?It] was the bishop's mark. St. Philibert is [?it] buried here. We saw the upper room with 3 huge organ bellows, the crypt & the cloister. Lovely. And a beautiful "Brown Madonna", where I lit a candle for Olivia.
We wandered around the town - beautiful stone carved doorways & gutters (all clean) down the middle of cobbled streets.
We're now at St. Jean de Vaux. The sun is shining, although there might be thunder.
__Wednesday__
There was lots of thunder in the night. But th[e] full [?in] day h[as] had OK weather. Sometim[es]

sunny. We have camped at [?Port mirail] in the municipal park. Near the sports field. V. few people about. The [?Accent] [?acceuil] woman gave us the name of a local restaurant that had closed 3 years previously. A nice has-been town. We ended up having a good meal at le Grand Condé. The camp site only charges [strikethrough]£[/strikethrough] € 5.5. We just missed a huge rainshower. At supper we sat next to a man [?mending] leather machines - Louis Vuitton
__Thursday__ Royal wedding for Prince Harry which we are going to miss. (Here I missed out that we camped nr. [?Chinee] country)
We went the wrong way north & ended up driving on a beautiful road, past [?Bonneil] village. I'd like to go back & walk there, near Châton-Thierry.
On to a large Auchan. Simon had trouble getting more camping gas because of a stupid cashier. However we reached an earlier Channel Train with time to spare. Men were using a drone in different places. [?Walking] something. What? Why? A man stood with cones in a circle round him on different sites.
    We reached Roksell Farm
& William joined us for lunch. We spent a

couple of hours looking at [?stuff] hop picking machines. We are buying stuff including a plucker bank for £10,000. A nice man, Anthony, 28 years old. Very self-assured. His first cousin has inherited [strikethrough]the[/strikethrough] half the grand-father's land (originally 3 or 400 acres) but doesn't work as hard. Anthony grows other things besides hops, including onions. He has an amazing new Wolf (double wolf machine) & was taking the Germans to the airport.
    We decided to come back to the Plough campsite again in Stalisfield. — A good evening. Another good supper.
__Friday__ Sunshine flooded the camping van at breakfast. Off to Canterbury. 597 Augustine arrived. His tome was here for a while. 1170 Thomas à Becket murdered. Lovely window of Adam Delving. We liked the undercroft. Gormley [?floats] body sculpture of nails. Great Norman pillars & capitols. 2 columns from 601. Chapter house with good ceiling & 1896 windows.
    People were friendly & bus driver told us a better place for the van Park & Ride.

captions: [?62] murals St Gabriel's Chapel, Crypt, Canterbury Cathedral
pages.text:
[glued-in postcard photograph of mural] [?62] murals St Gabriel's Chapel, Crypt, Canterbury Cathedral

John & Kay were at Peter's house for tea. Dave arrived & they went out to supper so that Kay could meet John's medical friends. (Dave works on a [illegible].) Nana took Simon to an Indian concert.

Peter took the children & Eve to a party at Gerald & Emily Stocock's house. 2 Chinese daughters, People played music; Gerald showed me the dress description ready for the BBC of Princess Di's dress.
Sunday Peter & Simon went to Quaker meeting. Nana & Eve took children to a street party. In the afternoon we went to Dinosaurs & Best horses in Concert. Then Peter & children went to a leaving party in a pub. In the evening Rocky came to Supper.
Monday At 7.15. Simon, Peter & Eve left on pedal bikes for Central London for a talk on the Bavagad Gita. It was an exciting ride. Army of cyclists on Embankment going v. fast. The Breakfast. Then India & China in BM. In the evening we went to a talk by John Grey.  May 22nd.
Tuesday Eve took children to school with Peter. We called in for a pub lunch with Judith & Rosemary on the way to Kennels. Then home

Thursday May 31st.
5.15. We set off for the farm. Nina fell backwards off to top stairs. Somersaulted ghastly. But she was OK. Bryony gave us a good supper. We slept in the garage courtyard because wet, thunderstorm Friday Simon was in the hop barn. Then we admired Jamie's new mowing machine (robot). We stopped in Bromyard + went on to __Eardisland__. A very pretty village near Leominster. Black & white houses. Flowers. We wandered into a house drive of a very overgrown house, we thought it was deserted, but a very cross voice said "Who are you?" + we ran away.
Delicious supper in the pub. Steak & kidney pie with suet pastry + yummy veg. I ate too much + couldn't sleep.
Saturday
  We walked around Burton Hall. (Burr = fortified dwelling) + saw rows of apple trees - difficult to tell what they are from a distance. Very beautiful + verdant lanes.
[right margin, rotated:] Shobodon Church. 1750

captions: Shobodon Church. 1750 Ogive arches. lovely old Romanesque font with lions
pages.text:
Unfortunately the way back, footpath, was thro a field of cows. I was too nervous, so we walked the last kilo on a main road.
  When we got back to the flat, boggy campsite, yet another camping van had got stuck & Simon help push it out.
[photo caption, rotated:] Shobodon Church. 1750
Ogive arches.
lovely old Romanesque font with lions

captions: Ancient Chestnut trees on-route to Croft Ambrey the Iron Age hill fort.
pages.text:
__Croft Castle__
We arrived & listened to the Herefordshire Brass Band playing WWI Tunes & others. Gorgeous. Inside the house were nice portraits. They family suffered for supporting Elizabeth v Mary & being Royalists. We went to the tea rooms & I realised I'd been there with Bryony a year ago to the children's play areas.

We walked up the hill from 4.30 - 6.30. One of the best walks ever, the Iron Age hill fort. Huge. Amazing views. PenyFann, the Malverns. All round.

Now back in the campsite - golden evening sunshine.
Bother Father (WWI) & son (WWII) died in the wars. June 3rd
Sunday. The loo in the campsite is just a little portaloo with wash basin. It's very clean.
I and Tinder a walk & we set off to [illegible]

__August 4th, Saturday__

At 9 Simon, Eve & Tinder left for Hattie's & Ramsey's wedding at Farnham. A lovely family occasion. We left Tinder under some trees in Farnham Castle Park. Blazing Sunshine. The service & supper & disco were all in the church. Richard told a story about Hattie climbing a 7ft wall on her army course, helping a girl, so they didn't have to run the course again.

The morning after we went for coffee & cake at Richard's & Mary's. Great fun being with everyone. We took the scenic routes. Heavy traffic for part of the way back.

__Wed Aug 16th__

We left at 12.45. & went to Brook House Farm for Simon to check on hop-picking machines. Then we went to Legges [as] we'd failed to take stuff out of fridge at home. On to Rhayader in the middle of Wales. We are camped at __Wyside Club Site__, [?1/4] mile from town, right next to river. Lots of rain in night.

pages.sketch_desc: Printed/glued-in map labelled 'How to Find Us' showing roads around Wales with RHAYADER marked prominently. Shows A5, A483, A470, A458, A44, A40, A477, A4112, A4067, A465, A40 and other roads; key towns labelled include Holyhead, Betws-y-coed, Porthmadog, Machynlleth, Caerws, Llanidloes, Newtown, Welshpool, Montgomery, Llandrindod Wells, Llanwrtyd Wells, Builth Wells, Hay-on-Wye, Brecon, Talgarth, Abergavenny, Dickhowell [Crickhowell?], Ystradgynlais, Swansea, Cardiff, Pembroke, Knighton, Presteigne, Liverpool, Chester, Shrewsbury.
pages.text:
__Thursday__ Weather better than forecast. We walked to the town. The bin man was friendly but - people - when we were trying to get coffee - were not. Like France. Take it or leave it. A most interesting building - Lost Ark - being refurbished as a live arts venue by a school teaching family from Telford. An interesting clock in the middle of town. Appalling traffic - huge lorries with logs. St. Clements church 1700s. We had supper at the Eagle - good steak & kidney pudding.

Friday
A cold, chilly day, overcast. But at least it has not rained. We drove up to the 3rd lake in the Elan Valley, cycled down to the 2nd dam (which we reached 2 years ago). It was a nice ride -

back to the visitors' centre & had lunch & looked at the exhibitions. Back at the campsite we relaxed.

__Saturday__
The campsite has people our age on cycling holidays (going to the coast) young mountain bikes, older motorcyclists, young mountain motorcyclists. We set off on our bikes to the dam (about 4 miles) Twider yanked the lead out of Simon's hand & chased sheep, got caught in bracken & re-captured. Bloody dog. We picked blackberries. In the van on the way to the farm now to pick up the smalls & keep them at 86 until Tuesday.

Aug 26th __Saturday~~ Sunday
Simon & Eve at farm for hop-picking. Parked in courtyard. Came home. Set let [?]

Friday 28th Sept  4 pm
  Simon, Eve, Tinder → __Dornston__.
Campsite with Fort Laramie + tilted horse! Lots of trees + flat but glorious sunshine + walk. We saw St. Nicholas church. V. old buttresses. There will be a WW1 [?tedoro] lecture on the 6 mo. when Alexander Kirby was killed. (Nov 9th). Peaceful walk.

Sat 29th
Night freezing cold. Tummy ache. Old pâté or beans? Heat has built up during the day. Another super walk + blackberrying + damsoning. Beautiful houses. Sheep, cows, horses, Shetland ponies, Llamas. Heron.

lunch + home.

April 5th 2019
Simon, Eve + Tinder come to near Barnsley. Arrived 5:30. Stoking[?field] new Cotswold stone shower block powered by wood stove.
15 [illegible]

captions: Newark Park, Gloucs / 16th century weathervane / on Tudor hunting lodge
pages.text:
6.45. Charles arrived. Supper in the van. Then we sat in Charles' van to warm up. Then we went up to toilet block to see warm community room with stove. Cold in the night. Horribly cold.     Ashen Mains campsite.

[photo of golden dragon/serpent weathervane against blue sky]

[rotated caption:] Newark Park, Gloucs
16th century weathervane
on Tudor hunting lodge

__April 6th__ Charles telephoned from next door van to say hello. His van's warm. Simon said we can look at new, insulated vans in autumn.
	Charles left his electronic boxes outside van + I tripped, barked shin badly. Blood absorbed into cord jeans. We drove to Newark Park. Still bloody freezing. I'm wearing thermal vest, long sleeved T, cardigan, aron cardigan, quilted waistcoat + coat, hat + gloves…. still cold. After coffee we sat in [strikethrough: so] sun + I need hat as sunshade.
	Amazing views from house. Tudor hunting lodge, extra building. Loos – long drop outside building + clever use of gutter rainwater to flush but waste. Hooks for clothes because ammonia from piss wd. get rid of bugs. Burning gorse in bread ovens raises temperature. Lovely plaster work. Texan Robert Power restored house 1970.
	Lunch back at van.

After a lunch time nap (Charles + Eve) + [?sunbathe] (Simon) Charles worked + Simon + Eve walked round a steep hillside to an area – Breakheart Quarry – owned by the Nuclear Decommissioning Area – covered in silver birch trees + primroses.
	We are off to a pub 400m away this evening, but [strikethrough: no] way, way down in the valley. The New Inn.
	It was only 400 m away, but one of the steepest tracks – door to door – that we've ever been on. The wood is called Kill End Wood. Was the mill wind at the top of the hill? Meal OK ish. Staff nice.
__April 7th Sunday.__
	Less cold. Charles left to measure hop [?with] for his computer on a farm near William.
	. We'd like to come here with family. It's less than 1hr 20 min. for everyone (except Ater – 2½ hours) Nearly in Heartbreak Quarry room to ride bikes. Campsite is a huge field

for flying kites. There's a big community room with wood-burning stove for drying out & keeping warm if the weather's bad.
	Simon + Eve + Tinder went to __Frampton on Severn__ to walk along village, fields, canal. Tinder v. good. Then on to lunch in a field. Then on to home.

Sunday April 28th
 We shd have left at 8.30., but we left 5 min late because TT, parked in front of van, was flat, so Simon had to recharge it. We are booked into a 10.30. tour of Dern Gate, Mackintosh's house in Northampton. We had wanted to camp this w/end, but it is too cold.
	We reached Derngate at 10 & went on a guided tour. The house was built 1816 & modernised for Bassett-Lowke by Mackintosh in 1916. The house cost ~~£820~~ £220 & the renovations cost £800 (workman's weekly wage £2). BL was colourblind & only saw yellow & black.

captions: 78 Derngate. Hall/lounge screen on side of stair
pages.text:
78 Derngate. Hall/lounge screen on side of stair

	On the way home we stopped with Judith Kirby for lunch at Bierton, then had tea on the roadside next to a field of sheep near Bicester.
